ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn entry exists for Burmese-style corn salad specifically. FOODON_03301710 (corn food product) is used as the closest parent. A more specific salad preparation node does not exist in FoodOn for this dish.
ontology.foodex2_description: Maize (corn) — raw or processed grain/vegetable product
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for Burmese corn salad. A00LC (maize/corn) is used as the closest defensible parent. The dish includes additional dressing ingredients not captured by this code.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept exists for Burmese corn salad as a prepared dish. SNOMED CT coverage of Southeast Asian ethnic prepared foods is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Burmese corn salad is approximately 200 g, equivalent to one restaurant plate as typically served in Myanmar cuisine restaurants in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Corn (sweet, yellow, cooked, boiled, drained, without salt) — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #11168; supplemented with USDA FoodData Central entries for dried shrimp, peanuts, lime juice, fish sauce, and onion for composite estimation
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for Burmese corn salad exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for the primary ingredient (boiled sweet corn) and secondary ingredients (dried shrimp, roasted peanuts, fish sauce, lime juice, onion) weighted by typical recipe proportions. Corn constitutes approximately 65% of dish weight; dried shrimp ~5%; peanuts ~8%; onion ~10%; dressing components ~12%. Values represent a composite estimate and carry medium confidence.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; sweet corn GI reference value 52 (boiled sweet corn, glucose reference)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal (peer-reviewed)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Burmese-style corn salad. GI of 52 is taken from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for boiled sweet corn (glucose reference scale), which is the dominant carbohydrate-contributing ingredient (~65% of dish weight). The addition of lime juice, fish sauce, and peanuts is expected to modestly lower the effective GI of the composite dish due to acid and fat content; however, the corn GI of 52 is used as a conservative upper-bound estimate. Glycemic load per 200 g serving = (52 × 15.4 × 2.00) / 100 = 16.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium from fish sauce and dried shrimp
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be aware that a 200 g serving of Burmese corn salad contributes approximately 620 mg sodium, primarily from fish sauce and dried shrimp, representing about 27% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg; portion control or requesting reduced fish sauce is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index from sweet corn carbohydrates
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a glycemic index of approximately 52 (low-to-moderate) and a glycemic load of approximately 16 per standard serving, Burmese corn salad can be consumed in moderated portions by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; the presence of dietary fibre (2.1 g per 100 g), protein from dried shrimp and peanuts, and acidic lime juice dressing may attenuate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Unsaturated fat from peanuts; moderate sodium from dressing
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Burmese corn salad provides predominantly unsaturated fats from roasted peanuts (monounsaturated and polyunsaturated), which are cardioprotective; however, the sodium content from fish sauce and dried shrimp warrants attention in individuals with cardiovascular disease who are following a sodium-restricted diet.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Burmese corn salad (ပြောင်းဖူးထောင်း, romanized: Pyaung Zu Htaung) is a traditional Myanmar tossed salad made primarily from boiled or steamed sweet corn kernels dressed with a pungent, tangy, and slightly spicy combination of lime juice, fish sauce, dried shrimp, sliced onion, fresh chilli, roasted peanuts, and coriander. The dish exemplifies the Burmese 'htaung' (tossed salad) tradition, in which ingredients are vigorously hand-tossed to coat every kernel with the dressing. The texture is a contrast of tender, juicy corn kernels against the crunch of roasted peanuts and the slight chew of rehydrated dried shrimp. The flavour profile is simultaneously sweet from the corn, sour from lime, salty and umami-rich from fish sauce and dried shrimp, and mildly spicy from chilli. Nutritionally, a standard 200 g restaurant serving provides approximately 236 kcal, 10.4 g protein, 9.6 g fat, and 30.8 g carbohydrate. The moderate glycemic index (~52) and the presence of dietary fibre, protein, and healthy unsaturated fats from peanuts make this dish a relatively balanced option compared to many fried snacks. Sodium content is moderate-to-high due to fish sauce and dried shrimp, which is a consideration for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The dish is gluten-free and dairy-free. It is commonly served as a starter or side dish in Myanmar restaurants and is increasingly available in Burmese restaurants in Singapore.

confidence_reason: No direct entry for Burmese corn salad exists in any primary regional database (HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for individual ingredients weighted by typical recipe proportions. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for boiled sweet corn as the dominant carbohydrate source. Confidence is medium because the composite estimation method introduces uncertainty in exact macro and micronutrient values.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for boiled sweet corn (#11168), dried shrimp, roasted peanuts, fish sauce, lime juice, and onion. No direct laboratory analysis of this dish is available in any approved primary source. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central source data for the component ingredients of this dish. 3. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported consistently across all component ingredient entries in USDA FoodData Central for this composite dish. 4. trans_fat_g is 0.0; confirmed zero as the dish contains no hydrogenated oils or ruminant fat sources in significant quantity. 5. GI of 52 is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for boiled sweet corn and applied as an analogous estimate for the composite dish; the actual GI of the dressed salad may be modestly lower due to acid (lime) and fat (peanut) content. 6. The cuisine_tags in the input JSON include 'Noodle' and 'Lor Mee', which appear to be erroneous tags from the field survey platform data aggregation and are not reflected in this record. 7. Halal status is marked false because fish sauce and dried shrimp sourcing cannot be confirmed as halal-certified without restaurant-specific verification. 8. FoodOn and FoodEx2 codes are closest-parent matches only; no exact ontology code exists for this specific Burmese prepared dish.

improvement_actions_taken: Constructed composite nutrient estimate from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for boiled sweet corn and secondary ingredients weighted by typical recipe proportions.

Documented all -1 sentinel values (vitamin_d_mcg, selenium_mcg) with explicit reasons in notes.
Confirmed trans_fat_g = 0.0 is justified (no hydrogenated oils in dish).
